channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nigerian Army Investigates Justice Crack Over Soldiers’ Welfare Complaints
item.description: The Nigerian Army don confirm say dem don arrest social media influencer, Justice Mark Chidiebere, wey dem sabi as Justice Crack. Dis na because some soldiers don complain online about how dem dey cho...

item.title: Tanzanian Arrested Over Murder of 11-Year-Old Girl in Kisauni
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has arrested a Tanzanian man, Anthony Juma, also known as Kipara, in connection with the tragic killing of 11-year-old Glorious Kaiza Maore in Kisauni....

item.title: Durban City eyes first Nedbank Cup trophy and R7 million prize
item.description: Durban City FC are set to make history this Saturday as they take on TS Galaxy FC in the Nedbank Cup final at the Peter Mokaba Stadium. This match represents a milestone for a club still striving to c...

item.title: Nigeria Customs Strengthens Western Marine Operations with Seven New Patrol Vessels
item.description: Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) don add seven new operational patrol vessels to the Western Marine Command to improve how dem dey fight smuggling and other illegal activities for waterways. The vessels ...

item.title: Four injured in Brixton drive-by shooting, one critically
item.description: Early on 2 May, a drive-by shooting occurred on Coldharbour Lane in Brixton, south London, leaving four individuals hospitalised, including a 25-year-old man in life-threatening condition. The inciden...

item.title: Government appoints two distinguished academics to Niti Aayog
item.description: The Government of India on Saturday announced the appointment of two eminent academics, Joram Aniya and R Balasubramaniam, as new members of Niti Aayog, as part of a broader revamp of the policy think...

item.title: NBI Recommends Legal Charges Against VP Sara Duterte for Threats and Sedition
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has recommended filing three counts of grave threat and one count of inciting to sedition against Vice President Sara Duterte. This recommendation comes afte...

item.title: Japan and Australia strengthen partnership with focus on economic and energy security
item.description: This year marks the 50th anniversary of the Basic Treaty of Friendship and Co-operation, known as the NARA Treaty, between Japan and Australia. Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi emphasised the im...

item.title: Narges Mohammadi Hospitalized in Iran After Severe Health Deterioration
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, the Iranian Nobel Peace Prize laureate and human rights activist, was reported to be in unstable condition in a hospital in Iran on May 2, according to a statement from a foundation ...

item.title: US approves $8 billion in arms sales amid stalled Iran peace talks and tensions in the Middle East
item.description: The United States has fast-tracked arms sales totaling over $8 billion to Middle Eastern allies, including Israel, Qatar, the UAE, and Kuwait, amid stalled negotiations with Iran. The sales include ai...

item.title: CM Rekha Gupta emphasises promoting Delhi as a prime tourist destination
item.description: On Saturday, Chief Minister Rekha Gupta highlighted the importance of rebranding Delhi as a key tourist destination rather than merely a transit hub. Speaking at a tourism stakeholders conference, she...

item.title: Potential impacts of reverting to inflation-adjusted capital gains tax discount
item.description: The Australian government is considering a return to the pre-1999 method of calculating capital gains tax (CGT) discounts by adjusting for inflation. Tax experts warn that such a change, if applied ac...

item.title: Celebrating Bibhutibhushan Bandopadhyay: Life, Writings, and Legacy
item.description: Bibhutibhushan Bandopadhyay, famed Bengali author of Pather Panchali, is celebrated through an exhibition curated by his grandson, Trinankur Banerjee, at the Kolkata Centre for Creativity. The exhibit...

item.title: Parañaque enacts law recognizing healthcare proxies for patients
item.description: Parañaque City has officially enacted the 'Right to Care' ordinance, becoming the third local government unit in Metro Manila to adopt this measure. The law allows individuals designated by patients t...

item.title: Atlantic Cod Populations Remain Critically Low in the Bay of Fundy and Scotian Shelf
item.description: Recent assessments of Atlantic cod stocks in the Bay of Fundy and the Scotian Shelf indicate that these populations continue to struggle, maintaining levels considered critical since around 2008. The ...

item.title: Highlights and Updates from the 152nd Kentucky Derby
item.description: The 152nd Kentucky Derby, held at Churchill Downs, showcased a thrilling lineup of horses, jockeys, and pageantry. Favorites included Renegade, trained by Todd Pletcher, despite drawing the challengin...

item.title: Election Commission orders repoll in 15 booths in West Bengal
item.description: The Election Commission ordered repolling in 15 polling stations in West Bengal after receiving reports of irregularities and complaints from the state poll machinery. The fresh voting was scheduled f...

item.title: Increasing Sox9 Protein Enhances Brain Cells to Clear Alzheimer’s Plaques
item.description: Researchers at Baylor College of Medicine have discovered a method to enhance the brain's natural ability to fight Alzheimer’s disease by focusing on astrocytes, star-shaped support cells crucial for ...

item.title: Four Dead After Gunfire During Community Patrol in Inanda
item.description: Police in KwaZulu-Natal are investigating four murders following a deadly shooting incident in Bhambayi, Inanda, just north of Durban, which occurred shortly before midnight on Friday. A group of comm...

item.title: Zanzibar to Host International Cultural Summit to Showcase Traditions and Promote Global Unity
item.description: Zanzibar is getting ready to host the International Cultural Summit from May 16 to 21, 2026. The event is organized by the UNESCO National Commission of Tanzania, in partnership with the Ministry of I...
